detaileddescription.para: Generated from class MR::MeshVoxelsConverter. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::MeshVoxelsConverter named voxelSize. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

Modifies a member variable of class MR::MeshVoxelsConverter named voxelSize. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it had previously in voxelSize.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_DestroyArray(). Use MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

memberdef.argsstring: (float voxelSize, float surfaceOffset, MR_PassBy callBack_pass_by, MR_std_function_bool_from_float *callBack, float offsetVoxels, float adaptivity)
memberdef.name: MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_ConstructFrom

detaileddescription.para: Constructs MR::MeshVoxelsConverter elementwise. The reference to the parameter callBack might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::MeshVoxelsConverter::MeshVoxelsConverter. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_MeshVoxelsConverter_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_MeshVoxelsConverter. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_MeshVoxelsConverter. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::MeshVoxelsConverter::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

Parameter xf is a single object. Parameter xf has a default argument: {}, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_FloatGrid_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
